name: Kalaloch Campground
highlights: Only campground in the park with ocean views
Sites above a wild beach — surf is your constant companion
Bluff sites are the ones to aim for
First-come-first-served for most sites
bookingWindow: Book what you can on recreation.gov; fill in with walk-ups
seasonalNotes: Year-round. Winter storms are dramatic from the bluff sites.

name: Hoh Campground
highlights: Deep in the rainforest at the end of Upper Hoh Road
Mossy, quiet, atmospheric
Walk to the Hall of Mosses before day hikers arrive
Elk are common visitors at dawn
seasonalNotes: Year-round. Rainforest is most atmospheric in winter — mosses saturated, river running high.

name: Quileute Oceanside Resort
location: La Push — directly on First Beach
highlights: Tribally owned and operated by the Quileute Nation
Directly on First Beach
Cabins, RV sites, small motel building
Sea stacks visible from your window — the setting is the thing
seasonalNotes: Year-round. Winter storms and summer beach walks are both worth it.

name: Lake Crescent Lodge
highlights: Historic park lodge on the shore since 1916
Lakeside cottages are the ones worth requesting
Dining room serves dinner nightly
The view from the porch is unreasonable
bookingWindow: Reserve well in advance for summer
seasonalNotes: Seasonal — typically May through October. Request lakeside cottage.

name: Kalaloch Lodge
location: Olympic National Park — Kalaloch coastal bluff
highlights: NPS-managed lodge on a bluff above the wild coast
National Historic Landmark
Rooms and cabins — bluff edge cabins are extraordinary
Creekside Restaurant on-site
The location is exceptional
seasonalNotes: Year-round. Storm watching from the bluff cabins in winter is singular.

name: Domaine Madeleine
location: Port Angeles — Strait of Juan de Fuca waterfront
highlights: Waterfront B&B with views to Vancouver Island
Five suites, French-influenced interiors
Exceptional breakfasts
Kayaks available
A genuine sanctuary for a rest day
seasonalNotes: Year-round. Five suites — intimate and quiet. Best for couples seeking a pause.

name: Sol Duc Hot Springs Resort
location: 12 miles into Olympic National Park on Sol Duc Road — no phone or internet service
highlights: 32 rustic cabins steps from three naturally heated mineral spring pools (98–107°F) in old-growth forest
No phone service or internet by design — complete disconnection inside the park
Hot springs heated by volcanic gases from deep beneath the Olympic Peninsula
Springs Restaurant serving farm-to-fork Northwest cuisine — open breakfast, lunch, dinner
Hiking trails depart directly from the resort, including the short walk to Sol Duc Falls
bookingWindow: Book through olympicnationalparks.com. 4–8 weeks ahead for summer. Open late March to late October only.
seasonalNotes: Open late March to late October only — closed in winter. No WiFi or cell service on property. Pool fee included in cabin rate.

name: Lake Quinault Lodge
location: 345 S Shore Rd, Quinault — inside Olympic National Forest, Quinault Rainforest
highlights: Built in 1926; President Roosevelt lunched here in 1937 — believed to have influenced creation of Olympic National Park
No cell service or WiFi — set on the shore of a lake surrounded by temperate rainforest
Roosevelt Dining Room with panoramic lake views; indoor pool and sauna
Guided Quinault Rainforest tours and boat rentals depart from the lodge
On the National Register of Historic Places — one of the great park lodges of the American West
bookingWindow: Book through olympicnationalparks.com. 4–8 weeks ahead for summer. Open year-round.
seasonalNotes: Open year-round. No cell service or WiFi on property. Roosevelt Dining Room closes periodically — check website before travel.

name: Colette's Bed & Breakfast
location: 339 Finn Hall Road, Port Angeles — 10-acre oceanfront estate above Strait of Juan de Fuca
highlights: Five suites on a 10-acre estate with 400 feet of ocean frontage facing British Columbia and the San Juan Islands
Gardens featured in Fine Gardening magazine — meticulously landscaped with winding paths and cedar groves
Multi-course gourmet Pacific Northwest breakfast included each morning
Every suite has a fireplace, two-person Jacuzzi spa, and private entrance from the gardens
Electric bike rentals on-site for Olympic Discovery Trail — trailhead less than one mile away
bookingWindow: Book 4–8 weeks ahead. Adults-only (16+). Two-person maximum per room. Check-in 4–7 PM only.
seasonalNotes: Open year-round. Adults-only — no children under 16. Gourmet breakfast changes daily. Summer lavender season in Sequim pairs well with a stay here.

name: Hoh Valley Cabins
location: 5843 Upper Hoh Road, Forks — heart of the Hoh Rainforest, minutes from the park entrance
highlights: Four modern northwest contemporary cabins in a forested meadow — elk are regular visitors at dawn
Private hiking trail on the property leads directly into the rainforest
The best base for reaching the Hoh early and beating the crowds
Family-owned by descendants of area pioneers; no pretension, genuine place
EV charging on-site; cedar deck with propane grill; full kitchenette
bookingWindow: Book through hohvalleycabins.com. 2–4 weeks ahead for summer. Often available midweek.
seasonalNotes: Open year-round. No WiFi on property. Cell service limited to Verizon. Elk most visible at dawn in fall and winter.
